clause_text: Subject to Section 2.4 [APPROVAL OF TRADEMARK USAGE] , Sponsor hereby grants Snap a non-exclusive, revocable nontransferable, royalty-free, worldwide license to: (a) use, reproduce, publish, perform and display the Sponsor Marks and Sponsor Brand Features on the Co-Branded Pages; (b) link to the Sponsor Web Site from the Co-Branded Pages and/or the Snap Web Site that contains the Co-Branded Pages and (c) use, reproduce, publish, perform, and display the Sponsor Content in and on the Co-Branded Pages.
risk_explanation: The License Grant clause presents a standard and balanced arrangement between the parties. The clause grants Snap a non-exclusive, revocable, non-transferable, royalty-free, and worldwide license to use the Sponsor Marks and Sponsor Brand Features, as well as the Sponsor Content, on the Co-Branded Pages. This license is subject to Section 2.4, which addresses the approval of trademark usage. The key characteristics of this clause are: (1) the license is non-exclusive, meaning the Sponsor retains control over the use of its marks and brand features; (2) the license is revocable, allowing the Sponsor to terminate the license at any time; (3) the license is non-transferable, preventing Snap from assigning the license to another party; and (4) the license is royalty-free, meaning Snap does not have to pay any fees for the use of the Sponsor's marks and brand features. These conditions suggest that the risk level for the License Grant clause is LOW. The clause is structured to provide Snap with a limited and controlled use of the Sponsor's intellectual property, while also maintaining the Sponsor's control and rights over its marks and brand features. The mutual restrictions and balanced structure of the contract further support this assessment. Overall, the License Grant clause presents a standard and reasonable arrangement between the parties, with no unusual or one-sided risks for the signing party, Snap Technologies, Inc.

CO-BRANDING AGREEMENT

This Co-Branding Agreement
June 8, 1999
Snap Technologies, Inc.

Subject to Section 2.4 [APPROVAL OF TRADEMARK USAGE] , Snap hereby grants Sponsor a non-exclusive, nontransferable, royalty-free, worldwide license to (a) use, reproduce, publish, perform and display the Snap Marks and Snap Brand Features on the Sponsor Web Site in connection with the logo link contemplated by Section 2.l, and in connection with its promotional and marketing activities contemplated by Section 2.5 [PROMOTION OF SNAP SERVICES] and (b) use, reproduce, publish, perform and display the Snap Content on the Sponsor Web Site or other Sponsor properties as mutually agreed by the parties.
During the Term, Snap will not grant any third party any right to sponsor any products or services in the Exclusive Category on or through the Snap Web Site.

Upon execution of this Agreement, Sponsor will pay Snap the amounts set forth on Exhibit A as the "Up-Front Fee" in consideration of Snap's development and integration of travel-related content for use on the Co-Branded Pages and/or the Sponsor Web Site.
The Term shall commence on the date of this Agreement and, unless earlier terminated or extended as provided below, shall end as of December 31, 2000.
Either party may terminate the Term upon not less than sixty (60) days prior written notice to the other party of any breach hereof by such other party.

In the event of termination or expiration of this Agreement for other than a material breach of this Agreement by Sponsor, upon notice from Sponsor delivered to Snap at least forty-five (45) days prior to such expiration or termination, Snap shall negotiate in good faith an agreement providing Sponsor with sponsorship rights similar to those described herein on terms and conditions to be mutually agreed upon by the parties.
right of any third party.

Features, along with all Intellectual Property Rights associated with any of the foregoing.

This Agreement shall be governed by, and construed in accordance with, the laws of the State of California without reference to its choice of law rules.

clause_text: Subject to Section 2.4 [APPROVAL OF TRADEMARK USAGE] , Snap hereby grants Sponsor a non-exclusive, nontransferable, royalty-free, worldwide license to (a) use, reproduce, publish, perform and display the Snap Marks and Snap Brand Features on the Sponsor Web Site in connection with the logo link contemplated by Section 2.l, and in connection with its promotional and marketing activities contemplated by Section 2.5 [PROMOTION OF SNAP SERVICES] and (b) use, reproduce, publish, perform and display the Snap Content on the Sponsor Web Site or other Sponsor properties as mutually agreed by the parties.
risk_explanation: The clause grants Snap Technologies, Inc. (the signing party) a non-exclusive, nontransferable, royalty-free, worldwide license to use the Snap Marks and Snap Brand Features, and the Snap Content, on the Sponsor Web Site or other Sponsor properties. This license is subject to mutual agreement by the parties, indicating a balanced and reciprocal arrangement. The signing party retains control over the use of its intellectual property, and the clause does not impose any significant or one-sided risks.

clause_text: During the Term, Snap will not grant any third party any right to sponsor any products or services in the Exclusive Category on or through the Snap Web Site.
risk_explanation: The clause in question is an exclusivity provision, which restricts Snap Technologies, Inc. (the signing party) from granting any third party the right to sponsor products or services in the Exclusive Category on or through the Snap Web Site during the Term. This provision is standard in agreements of this nature, as it aims to protect the interests of the other party by ensuring that the signing party does not dilute its exclusive rights. From the signing party's perspective, this clause is not overly burdensome, as it does not impose any significant restrictions on its business operations. In fact, it is likely to benefit the signing party by maintaining the exclusivity of the Exclusive Category and preventing potential conflicts with third-party sponsors. Therefore, the risk level for this clause is LOW.

clause_text: Upon execution of this Agreement, Sponsor will pay Snap the amounts set forth on Exhibit A as the "Up-Front Fee" in consideration of Snap's development and integration of travel-related content for use on the Co-Branded Pages and/or the Sponsor Web Site.
risk_explanation: The Revenue/Profit Sharing clause presents a moderate level of risk for Snap Technologies, Inc. (the signing party). Upon execution of the Agreement, Sponsor is obligated to pay Snap the Up-Front Fee as set forth on Exhibit A. This payment is consideration for Snap's development and integration of travel-related content for use on the Co-Branded Pages and/or the Sponsor Web Site. While the clause does not appear to impose any onerous obligations on Snap, it does create a risk that the Up-Front Fee may not be sufficient to compensate Snap for its development efforts. Furthermore, the clause does not provide any mechanism for Snap to recover additional costs or losses incurred in the development process. However, the risk is bounded by the fact that the Up-Front Fee is a fixed amount, and Snap is not exposed to unlimited or uncapped liability. Additionally, the clause does not appear to impose any severe penalties or restrictions on Snap's rights. Overall, the risk level for this clause is MEDIUM, as it creates a meaningful but bounded risk for Snap.

clause_text: Either party may terminate the Term upon not less than sixty (60) days prior written notice to the other party of any breach hereof by such other party.
risk_explanation: The Termination For Convenience clause presents a standard and balanced risk profile for the signing party, Snap Technologies, Inc. This assessment is based on a thorough analysis of the clause's text and its implications within the broader contract framework. The clause grants both parties the right to terminate the agreement upon written notice, provided that the notice period of sixty days is observed. This provision is typical in commercial contracts and does not introduce any unusual or one-sided risk for the signing party. The clause's language is clear and does not contain any provisions that would significantly disadvantage Snap Technologies, Inc. or limit its rights in a manner that is not reciprocal. Therefore, the risk level for this clause is LOW.

similar_clauses.text: Either Party may terminate this Agreement for any reason upon providing 180 days prior written notice to the other Party prior to the expiration of the then-current term or unless sooner terminated in accordance with the terms of this Agreement.

Either Party may terminate this Agreement for any reason upon providing 180 days prior written notice to the other Party prior to the expiration of the then-current term or unless sooner terminated in accordance with the terms of this Agreement.
Accordingly, either Party may terminate this Agreement or all/part of a Project for any reason and at any time upon giving the other Party sixty (60) days prior written notice.
Either Party may terminate this Agreement for any reason following the Initial Term upon ninety (90) days written notice to the other Party.
Following the Initial Term, either Party may terminate this Agreement without cause upon written notice to the other Party of at least 3 months.

risk_explanation: The clause in question grants a right to any third party. This provision is standard in contractual agreements and does not impose any significant risk on the signing party, Snap Technologies, Inc. The language is permissive, allowing for the existence of a third-party beneficiary without specifying any obligations or liabilities. This type of clause is commonly used to ensure that the agreement's benefits can be extended to third parties, such as investors or partners, without unduly burdening the signing party. In this context, the clause does not create any meaningful risk for Snap Technologies, Inc., as it does not impose any new obligations, liabilities, or restrictions on the company. Therefore, the risk level for this clause is LOW.

clause_text: NEITHER PARTY WILL HAVE ANY LIABILITY FOR, AND EACH PARTY HEREBY WAIVES AND DISCLAIMS, ANY AND ALL CLAIMS AND CAUSES OF ACTION AGAINST THE OTHER PARTY, WHETHER IN CONTRACT, TORT (INCLUDING, WITHOUT LIMITATION, NEGLIGENCE AND STRICT LIABILITY), WARRANTY OR OTHERWISE, RELATING TO ANY INDIRECT, CONSEQUENTIAL OR EXEMPLARY DAMAGES, IN EACH CASE, ARISING OUT OF OR IN CONNECTION WITH THIS AGREEMENT (INCLUDING ANY BREACH HEREOF) OR ANY OF THE TRANSACTIONS CONTEMPLATED HEREBY.
risk_explanation: The clause in question is a standard limitation on liability, which is a common feature in commercial agreements. It effectively caps the liability of both parties for indirect, consequential, or exemplary damages arising from the agreement or related transactions. This type of clause is designed to protect both parties from unforeseen or speculative losses, and it is generally considered a balanced provision that does not significantly disadvantage either party. The language used in the clause is clear and unambiguous, and it does not contain any unusual or one-sided provisions that would elevate the risk level. In fact, the clause is consistent with industry standards and is likely to be enforceable in a court of law. Therefore, from the signing party's (Snap Technologies, Inc.) perspective, the risk level associated with this clause is LOW.

risk_explanation: The Covenant Not To Sue clause presented does not pose a significant risk to the signing party, Snap Technologies, Inc. This type of clause is standard in contractual agreements and is typically used to prevent one party from asserting claims against the other. In this case, the clause restricts the signing party from suing the other party for any claims related to the features and associated intellectual property rights. However, this restriction is reciprocal, as it also applies to the other party's claims against the signing party. The text of the clause does not indicate any one-sided IP transfers, uncapped liability, or mutual restrictions that would suggest a higher risk level. Therefore, the risk level for this clause is LOW.

clause_text: Neither party may assign this Agreement or any of its rights or delegate any of its duties under this Agreement without the prior written consent of the other party, not to be unreasonably withheld; except that either party may, without the other party's consent, assign this Agreement or any of its rights or delegate any of its duties under this Agreement: (a) to any corporate affiliate of such party; or (b) to any purchaser of all or substantially all of such party's assets or to any successor by way of merger, consolidation or similar transaction.

However, either party may assign the Agreement to a corporate affiliate or a purchaser of their assets without the other party's consent. This mutual restriction and the exceptions provided suggest that the clause is standard and balanced, limiting the risk for the signing party.

clause_text: This Agreement shall be governed by, and construed in accordance with, the laws of the State of California without reference to its choice of law rules.
risk_explanation: The Governing Law clause in this Agreement specifies that it shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of California. This is a standard choice-of-law provision, which is commonly found in commercial agreements. The clause does not impose any unusual or onerous jurisdictional burdens on the signing party, Snap Technologies, Inc. In fact, it simply designates California law as the applicable law, without reference to its choice of law rules. This means that any disputes arising from the Agreement will be resolved in accordance with California law, which is a well-established and predictable legal framework. Overall, this clause presents a low risk to the signing party, as it does not create any significant or one-sided obligations or liabilities.

The clause requires the Sponsor to ensure that users who connect from the Snap site are at least one click away from any page that mentions a specific topic. This restriction creates a risk for Snap, as it may limit its ability to promote or discuss the topic freely. However, the risk is bounded in that it is a specific and measurable requirement, and Snap can take steps to comply with it. The clause does not appear to create one-sided or unlimited liability, nor does it severely penalize Snap for non-compliance. Therefore, the risk level is classified as MEDIUM.
